table_description: The buildings table contains information about various buildings, including their location, facility type, and other relevant details. It can be used for analytical purposes such as location analysis and facility management.

columns.name: building_eid
columns.description: The building_eid column contains a unique identifier for each building, which can be used to uniquely identify records.

columns.name: building_number
columns.description: The building_number column stores the building number, which can be used to identify a specific building within a larger complex or area.

columns.name: building_name
columns.description: The building_name column contains the name of the building, which can be used for identification and analytical purposes.

columns.name: building_facility_type
columns.description: The building_facility_type column stores the type of facility, such as residential or commercial, which can be used for categorization and analysis.

columns.name: building_ownership_type
columns.description: The building_ownership_type column contains information about the ownership type, which can be used to understand the ownership structure of the building.

columns.name: building_full_address
columns.description: The building_full_address column stores the full address of the building, which can be used for location analysis and mapping purposes.

columns.name: city
columns.description: The city column contains the city where the building is located, which can be used for geographical analysis and filtering.

columns.name: state
columns.description: The state column stores the state where the building is located, which can be used for geographical analysis and filtering.

columns.name: zip_code
columns.description: The zip_code column uniquely identifies records and contains the zip code of the building, which can be used for location analysis and filtering.

columns.name: building_latitude
columns.description: The building_latitude column stores the latitude of the building, which can be used for geographical analysis and mapping purposes.

columns.name: building_longitude
columns.description: The building_longitude column contains the longitude of the building, which can be used for geographical analysis and mapping purposes.

columns.name: active_in
columns.description: The active_in column indicates whether the building is active, which can be used for filtering and analysis of active buildings.

columns.name: active_units_count
columns.description: The active_units_count column stores the number of active units in the building, which can be used for analysis of building capacity and utilization.

columns.name: unit_count
columns.description: The unit_count column contains the total number of units in the building, which can be used for analysis of building capacity and size.

columns.name: is_single_elevator
columns.description: The is_single_elevator column indicates whether the building has a single elevator, which can be used for analysis of building amenities and accessibility.

columns.name: geo_type
columns.description: The geo_type column stores the geographical type of the building, which can be used for categorization and analysis of building locations.

columns.name: geo_price
columns.description: The geo_price column contains the geographical price of the building, which can be used for analysis of building values and pricing trends.

columns.name: update_date
columns.description: The update_date column stores the date when the building information was last updated, which can be used for tracking changes and data freshness.

columns.name: building_row_number
columns.description: The building_row_number column contains a row number for the building, which can be used for sorting and indexing purposes.
